world.headline: Trump Tells Netanyahu Not to Retaliate After Iran Missile Strike

main.text: 🚨🚨President Trump tells me: I am going to call Netanyahu right now and tell him not to strike back
main.note: Axios correspondent with direct Trump sourcing; widely corroborated by NY Post and Times of Israel

perspectives.text: EXCLUSIVE: According to a very senior official on the PM's team on the late-night Netanyahu-Trump call, the Axios report is inaccurate. Trump did not make personal remarks about jail or claim Netanyahu is hated globally. Instead, the tense call focused on conflicting social media posts...
perspectives.note: Netanyahu-team pushback on profanity-laced call reporting; single-source but from senior PM official

perspectives.text: This is Day 100 of the reckless war of choice in Iran. It's time for the Republican-controlled Senate to move our war powers resolution. So we can end this costly conflict immediately.
perspectives.note: House Democratic leader; partisan framing but accurate on war duration and war-powers push

world.headline: Trump Urges Iran to Return to Nuclear Talks After Missile Barrage

main.text: Spoke with President Trump. We'll have more on Fox News in a few minutes. "What I would suggest to Iran: You've shot your missiles, that's enough. Get back to the table and make a deal," President Trump told Fox News.
main.note: Fox chief foreign correspondent quoting Trump directly; aligns with White House rapid-response posts

perspectives.text: .@POTUS on a deal with Iran: "We're very close. We have a couple of points; they don't even seem like big points. They've conceded the fact that they will not have nuclear weapons."
perspectives.note: Official White House rapid-response account amplifying Trump's Meet the Press Iran comments

perspectives.text: This is Day 100 of the reckless war of choice in Iran. It's time for the Republican-controlled Senate to move our war powers resolution. So we can end this costly conflict immediately.
perspectives.note: Democratic counter-frame: diplomacy talk does not end unauthorized war

world.headline: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel in First Attack Since April Ceasefire

main.text: 🚨 The IDF identified missiles launched from Iran toward Israel. Defensive systems are operating to intercept the threat.
main.note: Official Israeli military account; confirmed by NYT, Reuters, and Al Jazeera

perspectives.text: BREAKING: FOX News' @TreyYingst spoke with President Trump after Iran's latest strikes against Israel. Iran launched multiple missile barrages toward Israel on Sunday, prompting air raid sirens across regions and sending civilians into shelters, according to the Israel Defense Forces.
perspectives.note: Major conservative outlet; pairs attack reporting with Trump de-escalation interview

perspectives.text: Breaking News: Iran fired missiles at Israel, the Israeli military said, the first such attack since a cease-fire paused the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran in April. Follow live updates.
perspectives.note: Mainstream outlet with live verification; underscores ceasefire breakdown risk

nation.headline: VP Vance Links UK Teen's Murder to Migration in Viral Post

main.text: Henry Nowak died the same way a civilization dies: abandoned, handcuffed by authorities who neither trusted nor cared for him, and accused of hate crimes he did not commit. His murder is as tragic as it is enraging. He should still be alive today, and he would be if the last few generations of European elites had stood their ground against the politics of self-hatred and the mass invasion of migrants, many of whom despise the West and the people who love it.
main.note: Vice president's opinion post; factual details of Nowak case disputed by UK authorities

perspectives.text: In his final moments, Henry Nowak told police officers nine times "I can't breathe" and four times that he had been stabbed. In response police officer dragged him across the gravel, handcuffed and read him his rights. It was the last thing Henry heard before he died.
perspectives.note: Right-leaning aggregator that drove the incident into U.S. discourse; video context Vance quoted

perspectives.text: David Lammy has revealed he phoned JD Vance to tell him he was wrong to blame Henry Nowak's death on the "mass invasion of migrants". The Justice Secretary and Deputy Prime Minister, who has a friendship with the US vice-president, said the amicable conversation had ended with the pair "agreeing to disagree"
perspectives.note: Reports UK Labour government's direct rebuttal; reflects center-left pushback on Vance's migration framing

nation.headline: Hunter Biden Goes Viral Clapping Back at Critics on X

main.text: @SoundDobad I know this may sound petty, but I can't stand it when people put photoshop a meth pipe in my mouth. A crack pipe doesn't have that little bowl at the end. This is why we can't trust AI. Please make the appropriate edit. Thank you for your attention to this matter.
main.note: First-person satirical reply; self-deprecating humor widely covered by USA Today and WaPo

perspectives.text: If you're out there defending Hunter Biden you have lost the plot! 🤣🤣🤣
perspectives.note: Former Trump campaign adviser; partisan dismissal without addressing substance

perspectives.text: Wait…Did he just say checkered past ? I'm 28 felonies, 6 bankruptcies, and an Epstein bromance short of his checkered past.
perspectives.note: Follow-up jab at Trump after president commented on his 2028 speculation; opinion not fact-checked line-by-line

nation.headline: Hegseth D-Day Speech Sparks GOP Backlash Over Immigration Remarks

main.text: Eighty-two years ago, Allied forces stormed the beaches of Normandy into the hell of war — answering the call of duty in defense of freedom. Today, we honor their courage, remember their sacrifice, and remain forever grateful for the liberty they secured for generations to come.
main.note: Official Pentagon post; separate Normandy speech drew bipartisan criticism for immigration parallels

perspectives.text: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's D-Day anniversary remarks criticizing what he deemed an immigrant invasion in Europe were "inappropriate," House Republican Michael McCaul said Sunday.
perspectives.note: Bloomberg reporting GOP foreign-affairs veteran's on-record Sunday criticism

perspectives.text: Q: "Would you still vote for FISA reauthorization if Pulte is in the spot?" Rep. Jim Himes: "If I had to vote again on this thing, knowing Bill Pulte's background, experience, and what he's done, I don't think I could look myself in the mirror in the morning and say this is a guy who should have control and access to this authority."
perspectives.note: Center-left outlet quoting top House Intelligence Democrat on same-day Sunday shows criticizing Trump national-security appointments

business.headline: Trump Says Stocks Should Rise After Blowout Jobs Report

main.text: BREAKING: President Trump says "stocks should go up, not down" after today's jobs report. "Growth does not mean inflation," Trump says.
main.note: Markets account quoting Trump; May payrolls beat was confirmed by BLS at +172K

perspectives.text: .@POTUS on a deal with Iran: "We're very close. We have a couple of points; they don't even seem like big points. They've conceded the fact that they will not have nuclear weapons."
perspectives.note: White House account backing administration economic and diplomatic messaging same weekend

perspectives.text: JUST IN: Another strong jobs report. The US economy added 172,000 jobs in May (way better than the 88k expected) and the revisions for April and March were 93k higher. Hospitality was the top job gainer (+70k) followed by local gov't (+55k). Unemployment rate in May: 4.3% (same as April). The bad news = wages grew 3.4% in past year. -->The lowest rate in 4 years and well below ~4% inflation.
perspectives.note: Navy Federal chief economist; data matches official BLS release with nuanced wage caveat

business.headline: U.S. Adds 172,000 Jobs in May, Crushing Forecasts

main.text: BREAKING: The US economy adds 172,000 jobs in May, crushing expectations of 85,000. The unemployment rate was 4.3%, in-line with expectations of 4.3%. April's jobs number was also revised UP by +64,000 jobs. This marks the second strongest US jobs report in 13 months.
main.note: Figures match BLS May Employment Situation report released June 5

perspectives.text: The government claims the U.S. added 172K jobs in May, twice expectations. Unfortunately, all of those jobs were either in leisure and hospitality, or in government or government-related services. That drives demand for imported goods, increasing trade deficits and goods prices.
perspectives.note: Perma-bear economist; sector breakdown is directionally right but inflation linkage is speculative

perspectives.text: JUST IN: Another strong jobs report. The US economy added 172,000 jobs in May (way better than the 88k expected) and the revisions for April and March were 93k higher. Hospitality was the top job gainer (+70k) followed by local gov't (+55k). Unemployment rate in May: 4.3% (same as April). The bad news = wages grew 3.4% in past year. -->The lowest rate in 4 years and well below ~4% inflation.
perspectives.note: Nonpartisan economist noting hiring strength alongside stagnant real wage growth

business.headline: Cathie Wood Says Markets Misread Strong Jobs Data

main.text: The jobs report was a barnburner. Nonfarm payrolls increased by 172,000 versus expectations for 88,000, while prior months were revised higher by 93,000. Wage growth came in at roughly 0.3%. Yet the market sold off. In our view, the market is misreading the signal. It is assuming that stronger than expected employment and growth will cause an acceleration in inflation. History would suggest otherwise.
main.note: ARK Invest CIO opinion; payroll numbers accurate but Fed-path forecast is speculative

perspectives.text: US nonfarm payrolls 172K, Exp. 88K, above highest estimate
perspectives.note: Concise jobs headline; widely followed macro account, data verified against BLS

perspectives.text: In fact, even President Trump commented on the decline after the jobs report. Trump said "stocks should go up, not down" after today's jobs report. However, when you look beneath the surface, it's fairly clear that stock do NOT want a strong labor market over the near-term.
perspectives.note: Explains bond-yield-driven selloff contradicting Trump's public expectation

elon.headline: I didn’t think I was qualified — no one good would join a failing rocket startup
elon.text: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes I didn’t “think I was qualified”. There was no other choice. No one good would join a rocket startup as chief engineer that they thought was destined for failure.
elon.note: Primary account of stepping into chief engineer role at early SpaceX despite self-doubt. Illustrates the talent acquisition challenge for high-risk deep-tech hardware ventures and the personal commitment required to advance rocket reusability.

elon.headline: Hardware is hard
elon.note: Iconic short observation in context of SpaceX landing a massive $920M/mo compute supply deal with Google (110k H100-class GPUs). Highlights the real-world difficulty of scaling physical AI infrastructure at the same time as rockets — primary from the builder.

elon.headline: SpaceX started with under 10 people and no office furniture
elon.text: SpaceX was less than 10 people back then. We didn’t even have office furniture.
elon.note: Primary source confirmation of SpaceX founding conditions in 2002; tiny team, no furniture, mariachi party origins. Matches historical photos and public record. Direct from founder on the humble start of the reusable rocket company.

parentPost.text: SpaceX started with a mariachi band party in 2002.

elon.headline: SpaceX origin: $50M from PayPal for philanthropic Mars mission, not commercial
elon.text:
True, when we were stuck in traffic on the way back to NYC, Adeo asked me what I was going to do after PayPal/X and I said I always wanted to do something to advance space, but didn’t think there was anything private individuals could do. 

The origin of SpaceX was doing a philanthropic mission to get the public excited about life on Mars, so that NASA’s budget could be increased to achieve that goal. There was no commercial ambition at the time.

The $50M was from the proceeds of the sale of PayPal to eBay.

After learning more about the limiting factors for humanity in space, it became obvious that the issue was a lack of advancement in rocket technology, in particular the failure to develop a fully reusable rocket, without which expanding consciousness beyond Earth is impossible.
elon.note: Detailed first-hand account of SpaceX founding intent (philanthropic Mars excitement to boost NASA, $50M PayPal exit capital, pivot to reusable rocket as the core technical blocker). Corroborated by Adeo Ressi parent post. High accuracy and primary-source proximity on the non-commercial origins.

elon.headline: Hiding in plain sight
elon.note: Brief reply acknowledging that the xAI strategy (massive GPU clusters for training) was visible in a 2019 tweet. Context supplied entirely by the quoted parent post; verifiability high once thread is seen. Minimal standalone text so honesty tempered, but no inaccuracy.

elon.headline: True
elon.note: One-word affirmation of Adeo Ressi's account of the pre-SpaceX intervention attempt. The parent post supplies the full context of the original Life to Mars philanthropic vision and Elon's early determination. Verifiable via the referenced conversation; the brevity is typical but the embedded parent makes the exchange meaningful.

elon.headline: Read books, talk to people & iterate rapidly with hardware & software
elon.text: @AJamesMcCarthy Read books, talk to people & iterate rapidly with hardware & software
elon.note: Concise statement of Elon's core development philosophy — continuous learning plus fast physical + digital iteration. Repeatedly demonstrated in Tesla Autopilot/FSD and SpaceX Starship/raptor engine cycles; transparent personal operating principle behind the tech progress.

elon.headline: ASML should be treasured — arguably the greatest company in Europe
elon.text: @NUnl ASML should be treasured and supported. It is arguably the greatest company in Europe.
elon.note: Elon's assessment of ASML's critical role in EUV lithography for advanced semiconductors (key enabler for AI training chips, Tesla FSD hardware, and compute generally). Subjective 'greatest' framing but grounded in ASML's unique tech monopoly position; primary view from a major tech customer/peer.

elon.headline: Antonio's support for SpaceX: loan in 2008 when it looked like failure
elon.text:
That was a short-term loan when I ran out of money in 2008. He did not receive any equity for it. 

Antonio’s ownership stems from absolute support, even when it looked like SpaceX would fail, and many investments over 2 decades. 

One could not ask for a better friend. He is a great man.
elon.note: Primary source from Elon on SpaceX 2008 financing details and long-term friendship-based ownership. Directly addresses and corrects equity claims with facts. Excellent verifiability and accuracy from the principal; neutral on the personal support that enabled the tech development.

elon.headline: No university degree teaches how to build an orbital rocket
elon.text: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes There is obviously no “degree” you can get from a university that actually teaches you how to make an orbital rocket, as none of the professors know how to do it!
elon.note: Direct explanation from founder on why practical, hands-on engineering (not academic credentials) was required for SpaceX. Highlights the gap in formal education for frontier rocket tech and the first-principles approach that drove reusable launch vehicles.

sports.headline: Cowherd: Two minutes in. Germany scores. Stay positive. Stay positive...
sports.text: That goal didn’t count. No reason to get cynical. This is just a friendly. We are barely taking it seriously. Still just 1-0 Germany.

ifollow.justification: David Sacks: Thank you @sriramk for this generous post. It has be...
ifollow.note: Truth score on verifiability and framing of this single post.

topPost.text:
Thank you @sriramk for this generous post. It has been one of the great privileges of my time in the Administration to work so closely with you over the past 18 months. Your skills are genuinely unique: a rare combination of deep technical fluency in AI, sharp policy instincts, exceptional strategic thinking, and true diplomatic talent. It will be a huge loss for the administration, but I’m glad we’ll continue working together with you as an outside adviser.

We've accomplished a lot together, and this feels like the right moment to recap some of the key milestones:
* You co-authored the Administration’s AI Action Plan, our comprehensive strategy for winning the AI race.
* You helped drive the AI Acceleration Partnerships that are positioning the American AI stack to compete and win globally.
* You played a key role in the National AI Policy Framework (executive order and policy document), which is now the foundation for discussions with Congress on a national approach to AI regulation instead of a chaotic patchwork of state rules.
* You helped deliver the Preventing Woke AI in the Federal Government executive order, which ensures that federally procured AI systems prioritize neutrality and truth-seeking over ideological bias and capture.
* You advanced American interests at the AI Summits in France and India and through state visits to the UK, the Middle East, and beyond.

As AI Czar, it has been a huge honor for me to work for President Trump as he provides the clear leadership and vision for American AI dominance. His policies have put America in the lead in the AI race: supporting innovation, unleashing energy abundance, building out infrastructure, pushing back against unnecessary regulation, enabling American exports, and promoting re-industrialization. 

Sriram was a key partner in turning these priorities into action. And as he said, this was a true team effort with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, NEC Director Kevin Hassett, OSTP Director Michael Kratsios, Secretary Rubio, Secretary Bessent, Secretary Lutnick, and too many others to mention.

Thank you again Sriram for your service. You made a huge difference. I’m sorry to see you go but grateful for the work we’ve done together and looking forward to what comes next.

parentPost.text:
🇺🇸🚀 SOME NEWS: I'll be leaving my role at the White House at the end of this month. After a break I’ll be working on helping tackle some of the large challenges facing America on AI (more on that later).

It is hard to express how big a privilege it has been to serve the American people and how grateful I am to have had the opportunity to do so.

First and foremost, it has been an honor to serve under President @realDonaldTrump . Without his leadership, we would not be leading in the AI race.

S

free.headline: Andreessen on the "greengrocer lie" — why we chant slogans we know are false
free.text: Marc Andreessen on preference falsification: the 1985 Prague greengrocer parable, back again.
free.note: Andreessen unpacking Timur Kuran's "Private Truths, Public Lies" + Václav Havel's greengrocer parable. No speculation — maps directly onto modern forced public compliance.

free.headline: Boycott debate highlights free speech double standards on race

main.text: They’re out here boycotting Chinese restaurants and Asian nail salons over one incident that the owners had absolutely nothing to do with. Imagine if white people or any other race organized a boycott against black owned businesses after a crime made the news. It would be called a hate crime and lead every single news channel in the country.
main.note: Commentary on selective application of hate crime labels.

msm.headline: UK minister condemns Henry Nowak protests but attended violent pro-Palestine demo - two-tier hypocrisy

main.text:
Shabana Mahmood has CONDEMNED the Henry Nowak protests but she ATTENDED a Pro-Palestine protest in Birmingham which ended in violence and disorder. 
She has since deleted this video. Our Home Secretary is #TwoTier
main.note: Side-by-side evidence of minister's attendance at violent event vs condemnation of Nowak protests; primary video and photo proof. Legacy media avoids highlighting the inconsistency in 'two-tier' enforcement tied to ideological priorities.

perspectives.text:
POLICE REFUSE TO TAKE A KNEE FOR HENRY NOWAK !! 

Police response “I don’t want to”
perspectives.note: Video of police refusing to kneel for Nowak contrasted with other responses; factual differential treatment.

perspectives.text:
Hundreds gather in Perth, Australia, to honour murdered British teen Henry Nowak.

The West is uniting against two-tiered anti-white injustice!
perspectives.note: Counter-narrative on protests; acknowledges events but frames as exploitation without engaging primary evidence of the murder and policing.
